root_text.an4.26:1.1: “Ye te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū kuhā thaddhā lapā siṅgī unnaḷā asamāhitā, na me te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū māmakā.
root_text.an4.26:1.2: Apagatā ca te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū imasmā dhammavinayā, na ca te imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ viruḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjanti.
root_text.an4.26:1.3: Ye ca kho te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū nikkuhā nillapā dhīrā atthaddhā susamāhitā, te kho me, bhikkhave, bhikkhū māmakā.
root_text.an4.26:1.4: Anapagatā ca te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū imasmā dhammavinayā. Te ca imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ viruḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjantīti.

translation_text.an4.26:1.1: “Mendicants, those mendicants who are deceivers and flatterers, pompous and fake, insolent, and scattered: those mendicants are no followers of mine.
translation_text.an4.26:1.2: They’ve left this teaching and training, and they don’t achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.
translation_text.an4.26:1.3: But those mendicants who are genuine, not flatterers, attentive, amenable, and serene: those mendicants are followers of mine.
translation_text.an4.26:1.4: They haven’t left this teaching and training, and they achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.
translation_text.an4.26:2.1: Those who are deceivers and flatterers, <j>pompous and fake,
translation_text.an4.26:2.3: these don’t grow in the teaching
translation_text.an4.26:2.4: that was taught by the perfected Buddha.
translation_text.an4.26:3.1: But those who are genuine, not flatterers, attentive,
translation_text.an4.26:3.3: these do grow in the teaching
translation_text.an4.26:3.4: that was taught by the perfected Buddha.”
